stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
This indicates ongoing community interest and performance tracking for a major open-source language model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its application in large language model tasks.
The associated tags confirm its relevance within the transformers and LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Corey Feld
bullet_points: LLM-powered deep research agents struggle with reliably verifying the sources they cite.
Current methods (self-citation or standard RAG) fail to validate source accessibility, relevance, or factual consistency.
The paper introduces a novel source attribution evaluation framework using an AST parser to address this critical gap.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its deep relevance to modern NLP and ML infrastructure.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets and optimizes execution on Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using local or cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to deploy gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Course correction: Google to link more sources in AI Overviews
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is enhancing its AI Overviews feature by implementing more robust source citation.
This change aims to improve transparency and verifiability within Google's generative AI search results.
The update represents a course correction in how Google presents synthesized information from multiple sources.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method for video generation that allows for joint control over character motion and camera trajectory.
It enables the transfer of character motion from a driving video into a novel scene while offering per-frame control of camera parameters.
The method is designed to enhance artistic video generation by providing fine-grained control over cinematography and performance.

stories.title: AI Co-Mathematician: Accelerating Mathematicians with Agentic AI
stories.author: Pushmeet Kohli
bullet_points: Introduction of an 'AI co-mathematician,' a new workbench designed for mathematicians.
The system uses agentic AI to provide holistic support across the entire mathematical research workflow (ideation, literature search, theorem proving, etc.).
Key features include an asynchronous, stateful workspace that manages uncertainty and tracks failed hypotheses.

stories.title: ActCam: Zero-Shot Joint Camera and 3D Motion Control for Video Generation
stories.author: Baptiste Bellot-Gurlet
bullet_points: ActCam is a zero-shot method designed for fine-grained control in video generation, addressing both character motion and camera trajectory.
It jointly transfers character motion from a source video into a novel scene while allowing per-frame control over camera parameters (intrinsic and extrinsic).
The model is designed to be adaptable, building upon any pretrained image-to-video diffusion model conditioned on scene depth.

stories.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML
stories.author: Swati Gupta
bullet_points: The paper challenges the validity of current global LLM leaderboards, which rely on pairwise human feedback.
Analysis of 89K comparisons across 52 LLMs shows that the best-fit Bradley-Terry ranking is misleading.
The study finds that the top 50 models ranked by the global BT metric are statistically indistinguishable, with win probabilities barely exceeding 0.53.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of current retrieval-augmented agents, which treat information retrieval as a 'black box'.
Current methods rely on iterative, exploratory querying, mimicking a novice user rather than an expert.
The proposed 'Superintelligent Retrieval Agent' aims to improve efficiency by incorporating strong priors, reducing latency and unnecessary retrieval rounds.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local execution on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-weight models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code in a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: The new Wild West of AI kids’ toys
stories.author: Sophie Charara, WIRED.com
bullet_points: New AI-powered children's toys are emerging, described as a 'Wild West' scenario.
These connected companions have the potential to significantly alter childhood play and routines.
The rapid development has prompted calls from lawmakers regarding potential bans.

stories.title: Manufacturing qubits that can move
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The article discusses the technical challenges in manufacturing qubits that possess mobility.
A core difficulty highlighted is the inherent conflict between standard electronic manufacturing processes and the need for flexible geometries.
This suggests ongoing hurdles in scaling up advanced quantum computing hardware.

stories.title: Sony says "efficient" AI tools will lead to even more games flooding the market
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Sony anticipates that 'efficient' AI tools will significantly increase the volume of games released.
Despite the rise of AI-assisted development, Sony emphasizes that human artists must remain central to the creative process.
The news suggests a shift in game development scalability driven by AI efficiency, while maintaining a focus on human creativity.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel architecture for Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models.
Challenges the conventional MoE design where each transformer layer has its own isolated expert set.
Suggests that the rigid per-layer expert allocation rule may be overly restrictive, as evidenced by routing probe results.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation of LLMs: their inability to generate novel, valid, and challenging problems for training.
Introduces VHG, a new method that uses verification to ensure generated mathematical problems are sound.
Offers an alternative to expensive human expert involvement or flawed self-play methods for problem generation.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The research investigates the impact of using the same optimizer during finetuning as was used during pretraining for Large Language Models (LLMs).
The study found that full finetuning with the original pretraining optimizer leads to a better learning-forgetting tradeoff compared to other optimizers and LoRA.
This observation, termed 'optimizer-model consistency,' suggests a novel approach to mitigating catastrophic forgetting during Supervised Finetuning (SFT).

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established ground-truth benchmarks exist for a specific domain.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to formalize this evaluation setting.
Defines the necessary constraints (scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) required to make scenario-based audits valid deployment evidence.

stories.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
stories.author: Anshumali Shrivastava
bullet_points: Current retrieval-augmented agents often treat information retrieval as a 'black box' process.
Existing methods mimic novice searching by iteratively refining queries based on snippets.
The proposed advancement aims to move beyond this by incorporating expert-level priors for more efficient retrieval.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across different studies.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for focusing too heavily on action recognition while neglecting broader real-world applications.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon decision-making in LLM-based agents due to purely reactive methods.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework to incorporate explicit trajectory-level strategy into agentic RL.
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision-making processes.

stories.title: UniPool: A Globally Shared Expert Pool for Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Hong Cheng
bullet_points: Introduces UniPool, a novel approach to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures.
Challenges the conventional design where each transformer layer has its own isolated expert set.
Suggests that uniform random routing can maintain high accuracy, implying that expert capacity allocation can be shared globally.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation in LLMs: their inability to generate valid, challenging, and novel problems for advanced training.
The proposed method, VHG, aims to overcome the reliance on expensive human experts or flawed self-play paradigms.
VHG introduces a verification mechanism to ensure generated mathematical problems are valid, mitigating issues like reward hacking.

stories.title: Optimizer-Model Consistency: Full Finetuning with the Same Optimizer as Pretraining Forgets Less
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The research investigates the role of optimizers in the pretraining and finetuning of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The study observes that using the exact same optimizer during full finetuning as was used during pretraining leads to a better learning-forgetting tradeoff.
This method outperforms other optimizers and even LoRA during the Supervised Finetuning (SFT) stage, suggesting a novel optimization strategy.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the practical challenge of comparing LLM safety when no established ground-truth benchmarks exist for a specific context.
Introduces the concept of 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to formalize this evaluation setting.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
stories.author: Olga Fink
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of clarity regarding genuine progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG).
Current research suffers from fragmentation due to inconsistent evaluation protocols across studies.
Existing benchmarks are criticized for focusing too heavily on action recognition while neglecting other real-world applications.

stories.title: EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity
stories.author: Sewon Min
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inefficiency of monolithic Large Language Models (LLMs) where only a subset of capabilities is needed.
It critiques existing Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) implementations, noting that restricting inference to a subset of experts causes severe performance degradation.
The research proposes 'EMO' (Pretraining Mixture of Experts) as a solution to enable practical, modular LLM deployment.

stories.title: Verifier-Backed Hard Problem Generation for Mathematical Reasoning
stories.author: Ning Miao
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in LLMs: their inability to generate novel, valid, and challenging mathematical problems.
Introduces VHG, a new method that uses verifiers to ensure the generated problems are mathematically sound.
Aims to improve LLM training and facilitate autonomous scientific research by providing high-quality problem sets.

stories.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
stories.author: Michael A. Riegler
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of comparing LLM safety when no established, labeled benchmarks exist for a specific context.
Proposes a framework for 'benchmarkless comparative safety scoring' to allow for pre-labeling evaluations.
Defines strict conditions (fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, etc.) under which scenario-based audits can serve as deployment evidence.

stories.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
stories.author: Zhenfei Yin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of long-horizon decision-making in LLM-based agents, where current methods are too reactive.
Introduces StraTA (Strategic Trajectory Abstraction), a framework to incorporate explicit trajectory-level strategy into agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Aims to improve exploration and credit assignment over extended decision sequences in complex AI systems.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Optimization
stories.author: Graham Neubig
bullet_points: Introduces Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO), a new RL approach for training self-replicating agents.
These recursive agents can spawn and delegate sub-tasks to new instances of themselves.
RAO enables agents to scale to longer contexts and tackle harder problems using a divide-and-conquer strategy.

stories.title: Can RL Teach Long-Horizon Reasoning to LLMs? Expressiveness Is Key
stories.author: Abulhair Saparov
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in applying Reinforcement Learning (RL) to LLM reasoning: the lack of controlled, scalable training environments.
Introduces 'ScaleLogic,' a synthetic framework designed to independently control two critical axes of reasoning difficulty: proof planning depth (horizon) and logical expressiveness.
Focuses on systematically studying how LLM training scales with task complexity, which is vital for advancing long-horizon reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Min Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MASPO, a new framework for optimizing prompts in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS).
Addresses the challenge of aligning local agent objectives with overall system goals in complex collaborative tasks.
Focuses on the difficulty of jointly optimizing role-specific prompts across interacting agents.

stories.title: Why Your AI Agents Are Still Bottlenecked by HTTP (And What to Do About It)
stories.author: asterview
bullet_points: AI agents are currently constrained by reliance on traditional HTTP protocols for external interactions.
The article likely discusses performance bottlenecks when connecting agents to multiple APIs (web search, databases, etc.).
It offers solutions or directions for improving agent performance beyond standard HTTP limitations.

stories.title: An LLM Walks Into General Relativity - Lessons from a Devoxx Talk
stories.author: tasenikol
bullet_points: Addresses the fundamental issue of AI-generated technical content being factually incorrect.
Focuses on practical methods for correcting and improving the reliability of LLM outputs.
Draws insights from a Devoxx presentation, suggesting industry-level discussion on AI accuracy.
